run.title: Voice Memo ASR Bake-off
run.notes: Real Voice Memos sample used to compare the first local ASR baseline outputs. The clip is long enough to exercise chunking decisions and contains useful project vocabulary: Wispr Flow, Billie Flow, LLM, and MacBook.

asr_results.label: MLX Whisper large-v3-turbo

chunks.text: Okay, so I am just going to ramble for a little bit and see how good the quality of this
voice memo is, I suppose. So essentially, let's say that I wanted to build a
project where we were making a local voice clone. So similar to Whisperflow, but not quite Whisperflow
because we would call it Billy Flow, and the whole premise would be that it runs
using a local LLM on my MacBook.

asr_results.stitched_transcript: Okay, so I am just going to ramble for a little bit and see how good the quality of this voice memo is, I suppose. So essentially, let's say that I wanted to build a project where we were making a local voice clone. So similar to Whisperflow, but not quite Whisperflow because we would call it Billy Flow, and the whole premise would be that it runs using a local LLM on my MacBook.

review.summary: Best default ASR result. It is fast, coherent, and hears LLM, but still needs vocabulary correction for Wispr Flow and Billie Flow.
strengths: Warm runtime is practical for a menu bar app
weaknesses: Writes Billie Flow as Billy Flow

asr_results.label: MLX Whisper tiny

chunks.text: Okay, so I am just going to ramble for a little bit and see how good the quality of this
voice memo is, I suppose. So essentially let's say that I wanted to build a
project where we were making a local voice clone. So similar to whisper flow but not quite
with flow because we would call it Billy Flow.
The whole premise would be that it runs using a local LLL on my Macbook.

asr_results.stitched_transcript: Okay, so I am just going to ramble for a little bit and see how good the quality of this voice memo is, I suppose. So essentially let's say that I wanted to build a project where we were making a local voice clone. So similar to whisper flow but not quite with flow because we would call it Billy Flow. The whole premise would be that it runs using a local LLL on my Macbook.

review.summary: Good smoke test, not good enough for quality. It keeps the shape, but loses exactly the project vocabulary this app cares about.
weaknesses: Splits Wispr Flow into the wrong phrase

asr_results.label: Gemma 4 12B Audio

chunks.text: Okay, so I am just going to ramble for a little bit and um see how good the quality of this uh voice memo is, I suppose. Um so, essentially, let's say that I wanted to build a um project where we are making a local voice clone. Um so, similar to WhisperFlow but not quite WhisperFlow because exactly.
I've been not quite with the flow because, we would call it, Billy flow, um, and the whole premise would be that it runs, um, using a local LLM on my MacBook.

asr_results.stitched_transcript: Okay, so I am just going to ramble for a little bit and um see how good the quality of this uh voice memo is, I suppose. Um so, essentially, let's say that I wanted to build a um project where we are making a local voice clone. Um so, similar to WhisperFlow but not quite WhisperFlow because exactly. I've been not quite with the flow because, we would call it, Billy flow, um, and the whole premise would be that it runs, um, using a local LLM on my MacBook.
warnings: Original configured Hub id google/gemma-4-12b-audio does not exist; used public audio-capable google/gemma-4-12b-it.

review.summary: Completed, but not default-worthy. The public audio-capable Gemma 4 12B checkpoint ran, but chunk overlap introduced text drift and the path is far slower than Whisper.
strengths: Public Gemma 4 12B audio-capable checkpoint can run locally through Transformers
Correctly hears local LLM and MacBook
Useful evidence for the native-audio branch
weaknesses: The originally named google/gemma-4-12b-audio Hub id does not exist
Chunk overlap produced drift around the Wispr Flow sentence
Too slow and too fragile for the first app default

asr_results.label: Voxtral Mini 3B

chunks.text: Okay, so I am just going to ramble for a little bit and see how good the quality of this voice memo is, I suppose. So essentially, let's say that I wanted to build a project where we were making a local voice clone. So similar to WhisperFlow, but not quite WhisperFlow, because we would call it BillyFlow. and the whole premise would be that it runs using a local LLM on my MacBook.

asr_results.stitched_transcript: Okay, so I am just going to ramble for a little bit and see how good the quality of this voice memo is, I suppose. So essentially, let's say that I wanted to build a project where we were making a local voice clone. So similar to WhisperFlow, but not quite WhisperFlow, because we would call it BillyFlow. and the whole premise would be that it runs using a local LLM on my MacBook.

review.summary: High-readability lab candidate. It produces a clean transcript and gets LLM/MacBook, but the runtime is high and it collapses the project names.
strengths: Correctly hears local LLM and MacBook
Runs through the current Transformers Voxtral path on MPS
weaknesses: Much slower than the MLX Whisper default

asr_results.label: Parakeet TDT 0.6B v3

weaknesses: Writes Billie Flow as Billy Flow
Runtime is too slow for the first default on this memo

review.summary: Useful as an advanced mode, not the first default; source ASR errors still leak through; keeps the important vocabulary visible.

recommendations.title: Default ASR: MLX Whisper large-v3-turbo
recommendations.body: It remains the best first app default: much faster than the native-audio alternatives, coherent, and correct on LLM. It still needs vocabulary correction for Wispr Flow and Billie Flow.

recommendations.title: Default cleanup: MLX local small text / light cleanup
recommendations.body: Light cleanup is the right first app default because it removes dictation friction without pretending ASR mistakes did not happen.

recommendations.title: Keep vocabulary correction explicit
recommendations.body: The report should keep raw ASR visible. Wispr Flow, Billie Flow, LLM, and MacBook are the terms to bias or repair first.

recommendations.title: Do not ship Gemma, Voxtral, or Parakeet as defaults yet
recommendations.body: They now run locally where public access allows, but they are slower and still miss the key vocabulary. Gemma also depends on the public google/gemma-4-12b-it checkpoint because the originally named google/gemma-4-12b-audio id does not exist.

asr_rankings.label: MLX Whisper large-v3-turbo
asr_rankings.summary: Best default ASR result. It is fast, coherent, and hears LLM, but still needs vocabulary correction for Wispr Flow and Billie Flow.

asr_rankings.label: Parakeet TDT 0.6B v3
asr_rankings.summary: Strong lab candidate. It preserves filler and timing detail better than Whisper, but is much slower and still misses the product names.

asr_rankings.label: Voxtral Mini 3B
asr_rankings.summary: High-readability lab candidate. It produces a clean transcript and gets LLM/MacBook, but the runtime is high and it collapses the project names.

asr_rankings.label: Gemma 4 12B Audio
asr_rankings.summary: Completed, but not default-worthy. The public audio-capable Gemma 4 12B checkpoint ran, but chunk overlap introduced text drift and the path is far slower than Whisper.

asr_rankings.label: MLX Whisper tiny
asr_rankings.summary: Good smoke test, not good enough for quality. It keeps the shape, but loses exactly the project vocabulary this app cares about.

cleanup_rankings.summary: Best default-style candidate; keeps the important vocabulary visible.

setup_findings.summary: The originally configured Gemma audio Hub id is not a valid public model identifier. The run used google/gemma-4-12b-it instead and recorded that warning on the ASR result.
Gemma 3n E4B is gated and was not available for this run.

lab_only_paths.label: Gemma 4 12B Audio
lab_only_paths.summary: Completed, but not default-worthy. The public audio-capable Gemma 4 12B checkpoint ran, but chunk overlap introduced text drift and the path is far slower than Whisper.
warnings: Original configured Hub id google/gemma-4-12b-audio does not exist; used public audio-capable google/gemma-4-12b-it.
